feed.title: Subject: Eltoo / LN-Symmetry on Inquisition Signet: BIP 118 + 3-Round State Chain (APO+CTV)

entry.title: Subject: Eltoo / LN-Symmetry on Inquisition Signet: BIP 118 + 3-Round State Chain (APO+CTV)
entry.summary:
In a recent discussion on the Bitcoin Development Mailing List, Sergio Demian Lerner raised several technical inquiries regarding the potential enhancements and necessities in the current opcode sets used in Bitcoin scripting, particularly focusing on the ln-symmetry CLN branch. Lerner's questions centered around the comparison and potential benefits of using TH (Taproot Hints) over CTV+CSFS (CHECKTEMPLATEVERIFY + CHECKSIGFROMSTACK), the specific structural role of IK (Input Keying), and whether the state binding aspects within script constructions fundamentally require TH or if CSFS could suffice independently.
Lerner is seeking clarity on whether the additional complexity introduced by TH provides significant advantages that are not achievable with the already existing capabilities of CTV+CSFS. He questions whether IK's functionality is limited to merely saving a public key push in transactions or if it plays a more profound structural role in the scripting language. Furthermore, he is exploring the possibility of simplifying the opcode set for implementing ln-symmetry, questioning the minimal set necessary for effective functionality which might lead to more streamlined and efficient script processes.
This inquiry highlights the ongoing efforts to optimize Bitcoin's scripting capabilities to enhance security, efficiency, and flexibility, reflecting the continuous evolution in blockchain technology and its applications.
